title: 1 Samuel: Samuel and Saul
objective: Deepen biblical-theological understanding of 1 samuel: samuel and saul and its place in redemptive history.

required_passages.text: Selected key passages for this unit segment, read in full during lesson delivery.

segments.audio_script: Today we study 1 samuel: samuel and saul and trace God's covenant faithfulness.
Listen to the assigned passage and note covenant themes, divine actions, and human response.
We place this text in its historical setting, canonical location, and literary flow.
We analyze theological meaning, covenant development, and connections to the wider biblical story.
Key themes include God's holiness, covenant faithfulness, judgment and mercy, and redemptive purpose.
Review question: Why did God reject Saul as king of Israel? Answer: because Saul disobeyed God's command to completely destroy the Amalekites and instead kept Agag and the best livestock.
segments.correct_answer: Saul disobeyed God's command to destroy all the Amalekites and kept plunder
acceptable_variants: he spared Agag and kept livestock against God's command
partial obedience in the Amalekite battle led to his rejection
saul kept the best sheep and agag disobeying the herem command

segments.audio_script: Carry this lesson into prayer, worship, and careful Scripture reading as we continue the course.

vocabulary.definition: The last judge and first major prophet of Israel; he anointed both Saul and David as king and confronted Saul with the famous declaration that obedience is better than sacrifice
Samuel's rebuke of Saul in 1 Samuel 15:22 — God desires obedience more than religious ritual; it establishes the prophetic principle that external worship without inward compliance is rejected
